channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dumped Furniture, 12th July
item.description:
On the grass verge opposite the nursery there are 2 dumped chest of draw units, 4 metal kitchen chairs and cardboard box.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 193, High Street, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tipsy bar putting chairs on tactile paving, 12th July
item.description:
Tipsy bar is continually putting chairs on tactile paving. They do not seem to understand what the paving is for and do not want to remove it. This is a constant occurrence. Please send your staff during hours it is open. This is a evening economy zone so attending during the morning wont help. Please advise them of the dangers to disabled people

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Millers Terrace, Shacklewell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Abandoned Blue-Lid Bexley bin, 12th July
item.description:
The bin has been abandoned for months and is now being used to dump waste and dog mess, causing a smell.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Longmeadow Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: Charity bag containing clothing which is spilling out., 12th July
item.description:
Noticed this bag was outside entrance to Barham Court flats in Church Lane earlier during the week, it has now gone walkabout across the road. Can you please collect and take away. Many thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Church Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London

item.description:

item.title: The recycling facility continues to be a flytip for dumping rubbish, 12th July
item.description:
Since Waldo Road facility has been closed the recycling bins in Magpie Hall Lane is now a site for flytipping rubbish. The amount dumped causes the pavement to be filled with rubbish, it blows across the road. I know because I litter pick, including this morning. Also notice rubbish is now flytipped in vegetation on footpath opposite. Two spots between first lamppost and second and from second lamppost and third post.on the path down to Trinity Primary School. Also noticed a horse in field playing with a long piece of plastic. Issue Reported many times over two years by many residents about flytipping at this "reclycing" spot, the reclying bins are left full for many weeks. I notice Council is investigating flytiping in Chislehurst, why not Bromley Common Magpie Hall Lane. The rubbish was taken away this morning by one of your teams, so many thanks to them but by next weekend it will be stacked high again.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Magpie Hall Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
